description: Citation-style coverage fixture. Each item is a realistic in-text citation as it appears after copy-paste from a manuscript. `expected` is the citation set RefCheckr SHOULD detect. `knownGap: true` marks styles we do NOT yet support — the harness reports these separately as a target, not a failure.

items.text: teprotumumab reduced proptosis by 2.82 mm versus placebo.1,2
response rates were higher with active treatment (1,2).
the p-value was 0.17 and the mean was 4.13 overall.
Smith et al. (2020) reported a hazard ratio of 0.80.
items.comment: Was a known gap; implemented via detectAuthorYear().

items.text: As shown by Jones and colleagues in 2019, survival improved.
items.comment: Was a known gap; implemented via detectAuthorYear().

items.text: efficacy was confirmed in two cohorts (Brown 2018; Lee et al., 2022).
enrolment began in 2019 and completed in 2020.
items.comment: Plain prose years must not be read as citations; author-year detection requires a name or et-al marker.

items.text: the trial protocol was amended (2020) before enrolment.
items.comment: A bare parenthetical year with no author name is not a citation.

items.text: the reduction was sustained to week 24.1
items.comment: A superscript citation immediately after a number (24.1) is suppressed by the decimal guard that correctly ignores values like 0.17. Genuine ambiguity; tracked as a target.

items.text: the ACR20 response rate was higher with active treatment
items.comment: Named endpoints with trailing numbers (ACR20, PASI90, EASI75) must not be read as citations.

items.text: patients with grade3 events and a CD4 count
items.comment: Letter-glued single numbers (grade3, CD4, type2) are identifiers, not citations.

items.text: these outcomes were consistent1,2 across trials
items.comment: A letter-glued list/range is still a strong citation signal and must convert.
